channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Can I get a tariff refund from DHL, UPS, or FedEx after the Supreme Court struck down Trump’s tariffs?
item.description: The Supreme Court struck down President Trump’s sweeping tariffs on Friday, ruling that he overstepped his authority in imposing them on global import...

item.title: Wall Street shrugs at widely expected Supreme Court tariff ruling
item.description: The S&P 500 was 0.1% higher in midday trading.

item.title: Trump revives alien debate with order to release government files
item.description: It’s been a big week for aliens in Washington. In a podcast that aired last weekend, former President Obama revealed that he believes in extraterrestr...

item.title: Reese Witherspoon says ‘don’t chase your dreams’—do this instead
item.description: We’re told from a young age to follow our dreams. But for Oscar-winning actress Reese Witherspoon, chasing your dreams is overrated. Instead, she reco...

item.title: Why urban planners should strive for ‘the photo album standard’
item.description: My family had Slide Show Night when I was growing up. Not every Saturday, but a whole bunch of Saturdays. Either my sister or I would be in charge of ...

item.title: CFO whose business fell 10% from tariffs cheers Supreme Court ruling: ‘No single person should have unilateral authority over tariffs’
item.description: Matt Totsch, the CFO of manufacturer Trim-Tex, said businesses like his have been dealing with crippling uncertainty caused by Trump’s chaotic tariff ...

item.title: Meet ‘Your Rich BFF,’ the former JPMorgan trader and TikTok star who wants you to talk about money on the first date
item.description: Vivian Tu, who refers to herself as “your favorite Wall Street girly,” also hosts the “Networth and Chill" podcast and published a book, “Well Endowed...

item.title: How Jeffrey Epstein exploited colleges’ ‘tremendous drive to acquire money’
item.description: Schools try to make sure there is no financial conflict of interest for the donor, researcher or institution. Emphasis on the "try."

item.title: Which Products Could Get Cheaper After the Supreme Court Tariff Ruling? Beer and Guacamole Top Our List
item.description: Everyday items like groceries could see price cuts, along which industries like housing that rely on imported lumber.

item.title: ‘I didn’t get a graduation’: How the Class of 2020 turned their pandemic loss into a running joke on TikTok
item.description: The Class of 2020 still aren’t over the COVID-19 pandemic cancelling their graduation. Apparently they never stop bringing it up, according to TikTok ...

item.title: Costco Made a Big Bet That Trump Would Lose at the Supreme Court. Here’s How It Could Pay Off Now
item.description: If tariff refunds materialize, Costco’s decision could look like sharp strategic timing.

item.title: After SCOTUS Blocks Key Tariffs, Here Are the Loopholes Trump Could Still Use
item.description: Trump could levy import taxes through four other trade rules.

item.title: Refund Chaos Looms After Supreme Court Tariff Ruling. Here’s What Companies Must Do First
item.description: Here’s what corporate leaders need to know about the billions of dollars on the line following the Supreme Court’s ruling against President Trump.

item.title: New York nurses union wins 12% raise, AI safeguards in a tentative deal to end monthlong strike
item.description: Over the past month, nurses had picketed during bitterly cold temperatures in what their union said was the largest and longest walkout in the city’s ...

item.title: The 7 Winter Olympics Ads That the Internet Loved and Hated
item.description: Some brands rose to the gold medal occasion. Other efforts fell flat with viewers back home.

item.title: Stocks up in final hour as Trump slaps new 10% global tariff after SCOTUS ruling
Will U.S. companies now have their tariff costs refunded? What we know so far.
